gaps.title: localStorage data tampering vulnerability
gaps.what_breaks: User can modify localStorage to change pricing before checkout
gaps.real_scenario: User opens browser DevTools, modifies parwa_pricing_selection to set totalMonthly: 1 instead of actual price, then proceeds to checkout with manipulated price
gaps.ai_agent_prompt: Write a test that verifies pricing data integrity between pricing page and checkout. Test should: 1) Create a selection on pricing page, 2) Store in localStorage, 3) Modify localStorage value, 4) Navigate to checkout, 5) Verify backend recalculates and validates pricing from scratch, not trusting client data

gaps.title: Frontend variants not synced with backend
gaps.what_breaks: Hardcoded variants in frontend could differ from backend, showing wrong prices
gaps.real_scenario: Backend updates variant prices but frontend still shows old prices from INDUSTRY_VARIANTS constant. User sees $99 but backend charges $129
gaps.ai_agent_prompt: Write a test that verifies frontend variants match backend API response. Test should: 1) Fetch variants from GET /api/pricing/variants/{industry}, 2) Compare with hardcoded INDUSTRY_VARIANTS in frontend, 3) Assert all prices, tickets_per_month, and features match exactly

gaps.title: No server-side price validation on checkout
gaps.what_breaks: Checkout accepts client-provided pricing without server validation
gaps.real_scenario: handleContinue() stores pricing in localStorage and navigates to checkout. Backend has no way to verify the prices haven't been tampered with before payment processing
gaps.ai_agent_prompt: Write a backend API endpoint POST /api/pricing/validate that accepts industry and variants, recalculates pricing server-side, and returns validated pricing with a signed token. Checkout should only accept this validated token, not raw pricing data

gaps.title: Missing input sanitization for 'Others' industry fields
gaps.what_breaks: Industry name, company name, website fields accept unsanitized input
gaps.real_scenario: User enters '<script>alert("xss")</script>' as company name. When this data is displayed later in the dashboard, it could execute malicious JavaScript
gaps.ai_agent_prompt: Write a test that verifies XSS protection for 'Others' industry form. Test should: 1) Enter HTML/script tags in industry name, company name, website fields, 2) Submit form, 3) Verify data is sanitized before storage, 4) Verify data is escaped when displayed

gaps.title: Website URL validation missing
gaps.what_breaks: Website field accepts any string, not validated as URL
gaps.real_scenario: User enters 'javascript:alert(1)' or 'file:///etc/passwd' as website URL. Could be used for XSS or phishing when URL is rendered as a link
gaps.ai_agent_prompt: Write a test for URL validation in 'Others' industry form. Test should: 1) Enter various invalid URLs (javascript:, file:, data:, relative paths), 2) Submit form, 3) Verify validation rejects non-http(s) URLs, 4) Verify valid https:// URLs are accepted

gaps.title: No rate limiting on pricing API endpoints
gaps.what_breaks: Pricing API can be scraped or abused without rate limiting
gaps.real_scenario: Attacker makes 10000 requests/second to /api/pricing/calculate, potentially causing resource exhaustion or scraping pricing data
gaps.ai_agent_prompt: Write a test that verifies rate limiting on pricing endpoints. Test should: 1) Make rapid requests to GET /api/pricing/industries, GET /api/pricing/variants/{industry}, POST /api/pricing/calculate, 2) Verify 429 Too Many Requests is returned after threshold, 3) Verify rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining) are present
